text: The laparoscopic view shows the base of the appendix junction with the cecum appearing normal. The surgeon prepares to perform an appendectomy, carefully avoiding direct contact with the mucocille to prevent rupture.
The laparoscopic view shows the base of the appendix at its junction with the cecum, appearing normal. The surgeon prepares to perform an appendectomy.

The surgeon carefully manipulates the mesentery with the left hand, avoiding direct contact with the mucocille to prevent rupture. The mesentery is retracted back to the base of the cecum, with additional mesentery being dissected off the cecum compared to a standard appendectomy.

The surgeon uses their left hand to grasp the mesentery for manipulation, retracting it back to the base of the cecum. The mesentery is carefully dissected off the cecum, taking slightly more tissue than usual for a standard appendectomy.
The surgeon performs a partial cecectomy, ensuring the incision is away from the ileocecal valve, while removing the appendix.

The surgeon performs a partial cecectomy, carefully avoiding the ileocecal valve, and places the appendix into a specimen bag for removal. The trocar is upsized to facilitate extraction without rupturing the appendix.
The surgeon places the appendix into a specimen bag for removal, ensuring the trocar is upsized to prevent rupture of the low-grade appendiceal mucinous neoplasm.
